{"key_event":"Postwar federal state-building after War of 1812","reasoning":"The war exposed serious federal weaknesses, but its aftermath brought the Second Bank, protective tariff, stronger judicial supremacy, military expansion, and higher nationalist legitimacy, moving the national state into clearer moderate capacity. Civil society remained strong through westward white male suffrage, regional resistance, and commercial/manufacturing organization, though Federalist collapse, Native dispossession, and expanding slavery limited any net gain.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":6.9,"state_change":0.6,"state_power":5.4}
